title: F5 BIG-IP LTM Pool Member Selection and Load-Balance Failures (iRule)
dataSources: F5 BIG-IP LTM HSL via iRule for failed LB decisions, `sourcetype=f5:bigip:ltm:lb:failed:irule`
app: Splunk Add-on for F5 BIG-IP (`Splunk_TA_f5-bigip`, Splunkbase 2680)
spl:
index=network sourcetype="f5:bigip:ltm:lb:failed:irule" earliest=-4h
| stats count by pool_name, pool_member, virtual_server, host
| where count >= 5
| sort -count
description: Events from the LTM load-balance failed iRule indicate the BIG-IP could not select a healthy pool member—often because all members are down, monitors are flapping, or persistence and priority rules exhausted valid targets. This is a direct precursor to user-visible outages.
value: Shortens detection time for gray failures where VIPs stay up but no backend can serve traffic, reducing false negatives compared to simple ping checks alone.
implementation: Enable the HSL iRule that emits LB failure events per F5 and Splunk documentation. Map `pool_name` and `pool_member` to CMDB application components. Page on any sustained burst of failures; use drilldowns into `f5:bigip:ltm` monitor and syslog events for root cause.
visualization: Table (pool, member, VIP, count), Timeline of failures, Single value (failed selections per minute).

references.title: Splunk Add-on for F5 BIG-IP — Source types (LTM LB failed iRule)
Splunkbase — Splunk Add-on for F5 BIG-IP
